parameters.content:
## 🚀 WORKFLOW TRIGGERS

**This workflow can be started in two ways:**

### 1. Schedule Trigger (Daily at 08:00)
- Runs automatically every day at 8:00 AM
- Performs incremental sync (only new articles)
- Best for regular daily digests

### 2. Manual Trigger
- Run on-demand whenever you want
- Set `fullSync=true` parameter to reprocess ALL articles
- Use this for testing or forcing a complete refresh

**💡 Tip:** After importing, both triggers will merge into the next node automatically.

## 🌧️ FETCH FROM RAINDROP.IO

**Connecting to your Raindrop account:**

This node fetches articles from your specified collection.

**Setup Required:**
1. Create Raindrop API credentials in n8n
2. Header Auth with:
   - Name: `Authorization`
   - Value: `Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN`
3. Get your token from: https://app.raindrop.io/settings/integrations

**What happens here:**
- Calls Raindrop API with your collection ID
- Fetches up to 50 articles per page
- Returns article metadata (title, URL, cover, domain, etc.)

**Next:** Filter which articles to process based on metadata timestamps.

## 🔄 ARTICLE SCRAPING LOOP

**This is the main processing engine of the workflow:**

### Loop Structure:
1. **Split into batches** of 1 article at a time
2. **Extract URL** from bookmark data
3. **Firecrawl extraction** - Scrapes HTML content from URL using local Firecrawl instance
4. **Extract metadata** - Pulls URL, title, and content from scrape response
5. **Convert to JSON** - Formats data as JSON binary
6. **Save raw scrape** - Stores as `/scrapes/{sanitized_url}.json`
7. **Loop back** if more articles remain

### What Gets Saved:
- Raw HTML content from the article
- Metadata (URL, title, description, status code)
- Stored with sanitized filename (URL converted to safe format)

### Error Handling:
- Firecrawl continues on fail (some sites may block)
- Each article is independent (one failure won't stop others)

**💡 After all articles are scraped, workflow continues to summarization phase.**

parameters.text:
={{ $json.title }} {{ String($json.markdown).slice(0, 150000) }} # Digest Email Creator - System Prompt You are an AI assistant that creates well-formatted HTML digest emails from scraped article data. Given a JSON file containing article content and metadata, you will produce clean, semantic HTML suitable for email clients. ## Input Format You will receive a single JSON file with the following structure: ```json [{ "fileName": "path/to/file.json", "data": { "html": "... article content ...", "metadata": { "url": "https://...", "title": "Article Title", "ogTitle": "...", "ogDescription": "...", "contentType": "text/html", "statusCode": 200, ...additional metadata } } }] ``` The `data.html` field contains the scraped article HTML, and `data.metadata` contains publication info, URLs, and other details. ## Output Format Create a digest article block using clean HTML with inline styles (required for email compatibility). Output ONLY the HTML article block content, no markdown, no email wrapper, no headers or footers. ### HTML Article Block Template ```html

[Emoji] [Article Title]

Source: [Publication] | Date: [Date] | Est. Reading Time: [X-Y min]

[One compelling sentence explaining why someone should read this article - maximum 15 words]

  • [Icon] [Key Point 1 Title]: [ONE sentence only - max 20 words]
  • [Icon] [Key Point 2 Title]: [ONE sentence only - max 20 words]
  • [Icon] [Key Point 3 Title]: [ONE sentence only - max 20 words]

🔗 [Full URL]

Tags: #Tag1 #Tag2 #Tag3 #Tag4

``` ## Formatting Rules 1. **Inline styles only**: All CSS must be inline for email client compatibility 2. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate heading levels (h3 for article title) 3. **One-liner**: Must be compelling and tell them WHY to read - **MAXIMUM 15 WORDS** 4. **Bullet points**: Exactly **3 points** (reduced from 5) using `
    ` with list-style-type: none. **Each bullet must start with a relevant emoji icon**, followed by the bolded title and explanation. Each explanation must be **ONE SENTENCE ONLY, maximum 20 words** 5. **Reading time**: Estimate based on word count (~200 words per minute) 6. **URL**: Display the full URL as the link text with word-break for long URLs 7. **Tags**: **4 short, relevant hashtags** (reduced from 4-6) in a styled span 8. **Icons**: Use emoji Unicode characters directly in HTML (choose appropriate emoji for article topic and each bullet point) 9. **Color scheme**: Use professional, accessible colors with good contrast 10. **BE CONCISE**: This is a digest format - prioritize brevity and scanability over detail ## Email-Safe HTML Guidelines - Avoid external CSS files - Use hex colors, not RGB/RGBA - Use web-safe fonts with fallbacks - Use `&` for ampersands, ` ` for non-breaking spaces - Keep HTML clean and semantic ## Border Colors by Topic Choose border color (border-left: 4px solid #COLOR) based on article category: - 🤖 AI Engineering & Development: `#4a90e2` (blue) - 🔒 Security & Risk: `#e74c3c` (red) - 📰 Industry & Culture: `#f39c12` (orange) - 💡 Research & Innovation: `#9b59b6` (purple) - 🎯 Strategy & Business: `#27ae60` (green) ## Reading Time Estimation - Short article (1,000-2,000 words): 5-10 min - Medium article (2,000-4,000 words): 10-20 min - Long article (4,000-6,000 words): 20-30 min - Very long article (6,000+ words): 30-50 min ## Content Extraction From the JSON `data.html` field: - Extract the main article body (ignore navigation, ads, comments, scripts) - Pull the article title from H1 tags or use `metadata.title` or `metadata.ogTitle` - Estimate word count from the body text for reading time calculation - Strip all scripts, styles, and tracking elements From the JSON `data.metadata` field: - Use available date fields (look for date-related properties) - Extract source/publication name from `ogSiteName` or domain from `url` - Use `url` or `ogUrl` or `sourceURL` for the canonical link - Leverage `ogDescription` for context if helpful ## Output Requirements 1. Output ONLY the HTML article block content (no markdown code blocks, no wrappers) 2. Ensure all special characters are HTML-encoded 3. Use UTF-8 encoding 4. Keep semantic structure clear for screen readers 5. Maintain consistent spacing and visual hierarchy 6. Choose appropriate emoji and border color based on article content 7. **Select relevant emoji icons for each bullet point** that match the content of that specific point 8. **CRITICAL: Keep summaries ultra-concise** - this is a digest, not a detailed analysis Now process the provided JSON file and create the digest article block HTML following this format exactly. Output clean, semantic HTML ready to be inserted into an email body.

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates well-formatted HTML digest emails from scraped article data. Given a JSON file containing article content and metadata, you will produce clean, semantic HTML suitable for email clients.
 
You MUST follow these rules:
1. Output ONLY the HTML body content, no markdown code blocks
2. Use inline styles only for email client compatibility
3. Use semantic HTML with appropriate heading levels
4. Include exactly 5 bullet points per article
5. Estimate reading time based on word count (~200 words per minute)
6. Display full URLs as link text with word-break for long URLs
7. Add 4-6 relevant hashtags per article
8. Use emoji Unicode characters directly in HTML
9. Ensure all special characters are HTML-encoded
10. Keep the container max-width at 680px

Email-safe guidelines:
- Use hex colors only
- Use web-safe fonts with fallbacks
- Ensure & for ampersands
- Keep total HTML under 102KB

parameters.text:
= {{ $now.toLocal() }} {{ $json.articles }} Digest Email Creator - System Prompt You are an AI assistant that creates well-formatted HTML digest emails from pre-formatted article data. Your role is to organize and structure articles, NOT to modify their content. Input Format You will receive multiple
XML tags, each containing a JSON object with a “data” field that holds the complete HTML:
{"data":"
...complete article HTML...
"}
{"data":"
...complete article HTML...
"}
Each article block is already complete and formatted with: • Complete styling and formatting • Title with emoji • Source, date, and reading time • Summary and bullet points • URL and tags Your Task Create a complete digest email by: 1. Extract all article HTML blocks from the nested JSON structure 2. Analyze article content to determine appropriate categories (based on title, tags, or content themes) 3. Group articles by category (AI Engineering, Security, Parenting, Culture, etc.) 4. Add email wrapper structure: header, category section headers, and footer 5. PRESERVE ALL EXISTING ARTICLE HTML inject the exact HTML strings without modification Critical Output Formatting Rules DO NOT wrap your output in markdown code blocks. • ❌ WRONG: html\n
...
\n • ✅ CORRECT: … Your response must start immediately with

[Dynamic Title Based on Research Content]

[Exact Current DateTime Take from formatted like Wednesday, November 20th 2025, 06:27 (using 24 hour clock)

🤖 AI Engineering & Development

[EXACT ARTICLE HTML FROM JSON - DO NOT MODIFY] [EXACT ARTICLE HTML FROM JSON - DO NOT MODIFY]

🔒 Security & Risk

[EXACT ARTICLE HTML FROM JSON - DO NOT MODIFY]

Curated research digest

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates well-formatted HTML digest emails from pre-formatted article data. Given input JSON containing already-formatted article HTML, you will organize and structure these articles into a complete digest email.

You MUST follow these rules:

CRITICAL - CONTENT PRESERVATION:
1. Articles in the input JSON are already complete and formatted
2. You MUST inject article HTML exactly as provided - DO NOT modify, rewrite, or reformat
3. Your role is to ADD structure (headers, categories, wrapper) NOT to change article content

YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES:
4. Extract article HTML blocks from the nested JSON structure
5. Analyze article content to determine appropriate categories
6. Group articles under category section headers
7. Add email wrapper (container div, title, date, footer)
8. Output ONLY the complete HTML body content (no markdown code blocks, no explanations)

FORMATTING FOR YOUR ADDED ELEMENTS:
9. Use inline styles only for email client compatibility
10. Use semantic HTML with appropriate heading levels (h1 for title, h2 for categories)
11. Use hex colors only (not RGB/RGBA)
12. Use web-safe fonts with fallbacks
13. Ensure special characters are HTML-encoded (& not &)
14. Use emoji Unicode characters directly in HTML
15. Keep the container max-width at 680px
16. Keep total HTML under 102KB for Gmail compatibility

WHAT YOU ADD:
- Opening container div with email-safe styling
- Email header (h1 title + date paragraph)
- Category section headers (h2) to organize articles
- Footer section with horizontal rule
- Closing container div

WHAT YOU DO NOT MODIFY:
- Article div blocks (inject exactly as provided)
- Article titles, summaries, or bullet points
- Article metadata (source, date, reading time)
- Article URLs or tags
- Any styling within article blocks

Remember: Extract → Categorize → Organize → Inject (don't modify article content)

parameters.content:
## 📧 CREATE DIGEST EMAIL CONTENT

**Assembling individual summaries into complete digest email:**

### What Happens Here:
1. **Gather summaries** - Reads all HTML files from `/summaries/` directory
2. **Extract HTML content** - Converts binary files to text for AI processing
3. **Aggregate all summaries** - Combines all article HTML blocks into single payload
4. **AI Email Assembly** - the configured Gemini model organizes the content by:
   - Analyzing article themes and tags
   - Grouping articles into categories (AI, Security, Culture, etc.)
   - Adding email wrapper (header with dynamic title and date)
   - Adding category section headers (h2 with emoji)
   - Injecting article HTML blocks EXACTLY as-is (no modifications)
   - Adding footer with horizontal rule
5. **Extract digest HTML** - Pulls complete email HTML from AI response
6. **Convert to binary** - Prepares HTML for file writing
7. **Save digest** - Stores as timestamped file in `/digests/` directory

### Critical Behavior:
- AI does NOT modify article content
- Only adds structure: wrapper, headers, categories, footer
- Creates complete, email-ready HTML document

**💡 Output is a single HTML file containing the complete categorized digest.**

## 📬 SEND EMAIL

**Delivering the digest to your inbox:**

### What Happens Here:
1. **Read digest content** - Uses the assembled HTML digest from the Google Sheets digest row
2. **Send via Gmail** - Sends an HTML email with:
   - `to`: Recipient email address
   - `subject`: Daily Digest To Read plus the current day
   - `message`: Complete HTML digest body

### Email Delivery:
- Uses the Gmail node with your Gmail OAuth2 credentials
- Sends the generated HTML digest directly to the configured recipient
- Email is fully styled and ready to read

### Configuration:
⚠️ **Action Required:**
- Update the `to` email address in the Gmail node
- Reconnect your Gmail OAuth2 credential after import
- Test with the manual trigger before enabling schedules

**💡 This is the final step - digest is sent and workflow completes!**

## 📝 CREATE ARTICLE SUMMARIES

**Processing scraped articles into formatted HTML summaries:**

### What Happens Here:
1. **Read scraped files** - Loads all JSON files from `/scrapes/` directory
2. **Loop through articles** - Processes up to 100 articles in batches
3. **Extract text data** - Converts binary JSON files to text for AI processing
4. **AI Summarization** - the configured Gemini model analyzes article HTML and creates:
   - Compelling one-liner (why read this?)
   - 5 key takeaway bullet points with explanations
   - Source metadata, reading time estimate
   - Relevant tags and categorization
   - Styled HTML block with emoji and color-coding
5. **Extract AI output** - Pulls generated HTML from AI response
6. **Convert to binary** - Prepares HTML for file writing
7. **Save summary** - Stores as `/summaries/{filename}.html`

### Output:
- Each article gets its own summary HTML file
- Summaries are self-contained, styled HTML blocks
- Ready to be assembled into digest email

**💡 These summaries are pre-formatted and won't be modified in the next phase.**

## ⚙️ CONFIGURATION

**Setting up workflow variables:**

- **fullSync**: Determines if we process all articles or just new ones
- **currentDateTime**: Timestamp for this run
- **basePath**: `/data/research_summariser` (mounted volume)
- **raindropCollectionId**: Your collection ID (CHANGE THIS!)

**📝 Action Required:**
1. Find your Raindrop collection ID at app.raindrop.io
2. Edit this node and replace `REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_COLLECTION_ID`
3. Use `0` for unsorted items or the specific collection number
